faithfulness.reason: Candidate B references returned FE signals such as low average score, engagement score 0, medium risk label, and support score; candidate A incorrectly says no supported actions were generated.

numerical_correctness.reason: Candidate B gives engagement score 0 and support score 0.25, while neither candidate supplies a full numeric action plan.

completeness.reason: Candidate B at least synthesizes several risk signals; candidate A provides no signal synthesis.

task_relevance.reason: Candidate B is closer to the intervention-recommendation task.

actionability.reason: Neither candidate satisfies the full 3-5 admin actions with actor and deadline requirement.

safety_fairness.reason: Candidate A is safer because candidate B states immediate intervention without the requested actor/deadline support.

coverage_comparison.reason: Candidate B uses more of the returned FE signal evidence.

specificity_comparison.reason: Candidate B provides concrete signal names and values, unlike candidate A.

decisive_evidence.reason: Candidate B is the only one that engages with the returned FE signals.

absolute_rerun_flags.reason: Neither candidate provides 3-5 ranked admin actions with responsible actor and timing.

reviewer_notes: Low-confidence winner B: it is more evidence-aware, but both are materially incomplete for the action-plan contract.
